text: Hello, this is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 你好，这是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Neil.
zh: 我是尼尔。

text: And I'm Georgie.
zh: 我是乔治。

text: Neil, we're talking about protein today.
zh: 尼尔，我们今天要聊聊蛋白质。

text: What type of protein do you usually eat?
zh: 你平时都吃什么类型的蛋白质？

text: Well, I like all kinds of proteins – fish, bit of chicken.
zh: 嗯，各种蛋白质我都喜欢——鱼啊，还有一点鸡肉。

text: But I think I really like an egg.
zh: 不过我觉得我最喜欢鸡蛋。

text: An egg is my favourite kind of protein.
zh: 鸡蛋是我最喜欢的蛋白质。

text: I was going to say that too.
zh: 我本来也想这么说。

text: I'm a big fan of breakfast foods and eggs are my favourite.
zh: 我特别喜欢早餐食物，鸡蛋是我的最爱。

text: Well, you can't beat a good breakfast.
zh: 嗯，一顿丰盛的早餐真是无可比拟。

text: Of all the main food groups, such as carbohydrate, fat and fibre, it's protein that seems to be most in the news.
zh: 在碳水化合物、脂肪和纤维这些主要食物类别中，蛋白质似乎是最常上新闻的。

text: In shops and on social media, you'll find all kinds of high -protein foods being advertised, from shakes and yoghurts to chocolate.
zh: 在商店和社交媒体上，你会看到各种高蛋白食品的广告，从蛋白奶昔、酸奶到巧克力都有。

text: Here's Ruth Alexander, presenter of BBC World Service programme, The Food Chain.
zh: 下面是BBC世界服务节目《The Food Chain》主持人露丝·亚历山大带来的内容。

text: Cast your eye over most aisles in the supermarkets now and you'll see high protein products in abundance.
zh: 现在你随便看看超市里的大多数货架，都会发现高蛋白产品比比皆是。

text: Pasta made with lentils and chickpeas rather than wheat flour.
zh: 用扁豆和鹰嘴豆而不是小麦粉做的意大利面。

text: Bread fortified with beans and pulses.
zh: 添加了豆类和豆科作物强化的面包。

text: Ruth casts her eye over the supermarket shelves.
zh: 露丝浏览了一下超市货架。

text: If you cast your eye over something, you take a quick look at it.
zh: 如果你cast your eye over（浏览）某样东西，就是快速看一眼。

text: What she sees are protein products in abundance.
zh: 她看到的是大量存在的蛋白质产品。

text: In other words, in large quantities.
zh: 换句话说，就是数量很多。

text: products which already contain protein, like yoghurt, are repackaged as high protein, while other items have extra protein added, often in the form of beans and pulses.
zh: 像酸奶这样本来就含有蛋白质的产品被重新包装成“高蛋白”产品，而其他产品则被额外添加了蛋白质，通常是以豆类的形式。

text: Protein is big business.
zh: 蛋白质是一门大生意。

text: Our bodies need protein for health and to build muscle.
zh: 我们的身体需要蛋白质来维持健康和增长肌肉。

text: But with all this focus on protein, are we forgetting about other important nutrients, such as fibre?
zh: 但在这种对蛋白质的高度关注下，我们是不是忽略了其他重要的营养成分，比如纤维？

text: That's what we'll be discussing in this programme, hearing some useful new words and phrases.
zh: 这就是我们本期节目要讨论的内容，我们还会学到一些有用的新词汇和短语。

text: But First I have a question for you, Georgie.
zh: 不过首先，我有一个问题要问你，乔治。

text: The popularity of protein started with bodybuilders and people working out in gyms who ate extra protein to gain muscle.
zh: 蛋白质的流行始于健美运动员和健身房里那些为了增肌而摄入额外蛋白质的人。

text: Irishman Patrick Callaghan is a big name in the protein muscle world, but do you know who he is?
zh: 爱尔兰人帕特里克·卡拉汉在蛋白质健美界是个响当当的人物，但你知道他是谁吗？

text: he a the owner of a company which makes protein shakes,
zh: a. 一家生产蛋白奶昔公司的老板，

text: b the winner of the 2024 Mr Mr Universe bodybuilding contest, or c the scientist who discovered a new protein in outer space?
zh: b. 2024年“宇宙先生”健美比赛的冠军，还是c. 在外太空发现新蛋白质的科学家？

text: Ooh, I'm going to say Patrick Callaghan is the winner of the 2024 Mr Universe bodybuilding contest.
zh: 哦，我猜帕特里克·卡拉汉是2024年“宇宙先生”健美比赛的冠军。

text: OK, we'll find out if you're right at the end.
zh: 好的，我们节目最后会揭晓答案，看你猜得对不对。

text: Marketing campaigns that promote sales of high protein food are certainly working.
zh: 推广高蛋白食品销售的营销活动确实奏效了。

text: Sales of protein products make around £7 billion globally.
zh: 蛋白质产品的全球销售额约为70亿英镑。

text: globally.
zh: （全球范围内。）

text: Here's Scott Dicker from Spins, a market research company in Chicago, talking with BBC programme The Food Chain.
zh: 下面是芝加哥市场研究公司Spins的斯科特·迪克在BBC节目《The Food Chain》中的发言。

text: So sometimes carbs are good, sometimes they're bad for consumers.
zh: 所以有时候碳水对消费者来说是好的，有时候是不好的。

text: Sometimes fat is good, sometimes it's bad for consumers.
zh: 有时候脂肪对消费者来说是好的，有时候是不好的。

text: But protein seems to have this continuous health halo on it.
zh: 但蛋白质似乎一直笼罩着一种持续不变的“健康光环”。

text: Unlike carbs – that's carbohydrates – and fat, consumers don't see any downside to eating protein.
zh: 不像碳水（也就是碳水化合物）和脂肪，消费者似乎看不到吃蛋白质有什么坏处。

text: Scott says protein has a health halo, a marketing term which describes perceiving a food to be healthy based on a single feature such as being organic,
zh: 斯科特说蛋白质具有“健康光环”，这是一个营销术语，指人们仅凭某个单一特征——比如有机、

text: gluten -free or in this case high protein, even if its overall nutritional value is poor.
zh: 无麸质，或者在这里是高蛋白——就认为这种食品是健康的，即使它整体营养价值并不高。

text: Yes, food which used to be considered unhealthy is now called healthy just because a spoonful of protein powder has been added.
zh: 是的，一些原本被认为不健康的食品，现在只因为加了一勺蛋白粉，就被称为“健康食品”。

text: So could Could our modern obsession with protein mean that we ignore other important food groups like fibre?
zh: 那么，我们对蛋白质的现代痴迷，是否意味着我们忽视了像纤维这样其他重要的食物类别？

text: NHS surgeon and blogger Dr Karen Rajan thinks so and explains why here to Ruth Alexander on BBC World Service programme The Food Chain.
zh: 英国国民保健署（NHS）外科医生兼博主凯伦·拉詹博士认为确实如此，她在BBC世界服务节目《The Food Chain》中向露丝·亚历山大解释了原因。

text: And do you think we're paying enough attention to fibre?
zh: 你认为我们对纤维的关注足够吗？

text: Historically, probably not.
zh: 从历史上看，可能不够。

text: I think the tide is turning and there's more people interested in fibre and optimising their fibre intake and fibre maxing even.
zh: 我认为形势正在扭转，越来越多人开始关注纤维，并优化他们的纤维摄入量，甚至出现了“纤维最大化”这种做法。

text: It's a trend on TikTok.
zh: 这是TikTok上的一个潮流。

text: What is fibre maxing?
zh: 什么是“纤维最大化”？

text: Fibre maxing is, you know, as it suggests, similar to the protein maxing trend where people are trying to maximise their fibre intake with little hacks or tricks or tips that they could do,
zh: “纤维最大化”，顾名思义，类似于“蛋白质最大化”的潮流，人们试图通过一些小窍门、技巧或提示来最大化他们的纤维摄入量，

text: whether it's, you know, adding certain seeds or nuts to snacks.
zh: 比如在零食里加入某些种子或坚果。

text: Many people pay more attention to protein than fibre in their diet.
zh: 很多人在饮食中更关注蛋白质，而不是纤维。

text: But Dr Karan thinks the tide is turning, an idiom which means that things are changing.
zh: 但凯伦博士认为形势正在转变，这个习语的意思是事情正在发生变化。

text: In part, this This is thanks to a new trend from America called fibre -maxing – consuming lots of fibre by eating fibre -rich foods and supplements for their health benefits.
zh: 部分原因要归功于一个源自美国的新潮流，叫做“纤维最大化”——通过食用富含纤维的食物和补充剂来大量摄入纤维，以获得健康益处。

text: Dr Karan also gives some hacks – good solutions or pieces of advice to help boost your fibre intake, for example adding seeds and nuts to meals.
zh: 凯伦博士还给出了一些“hacks”——也就是能帮助你提升纤维摄入量的好方法或建议，比如在餐食中加入种子和坚果。

text: In fact, the healthiest diet is probably a balanced diet, one containing items from all the major food groups.
zh: 事实上，最健康的饮食可能是均衡饮食，也就是包含所有主要食物类别的饮食。

text: But I'm still intrigued by your question, Neil.
zh: 不过我还是对你的问题很好奇，尼尔。

text: isn't it time you revealed the answer?
zh: 是不是该公布答案了？

text: I asked you who the Irishman Patrick Callaghan was.
zh: 我问你爱尔兰人帕特里克·卡拉汉是谁。

text: And I said he was the winner of the Mr Universe contest.
zh: 我说他是“宇宙先生”比赛的冠军。

text: And you were correct.
zh: 你答对了。

text: Yay!
zh: 耶！

text: OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learned in this episode.
zh: 好的，让我们回顾一下本期节目学到的词汇。

text: If you cast your eye over something, you have a quick look at it.
zh: 如果你cast your eye over某样东西，就是快速看一眼。

text: If something is in abundance, there's a lot or more than enough of it.
zh: 如果某样东西in abundance，就是数量很多，绰绰有余。

text: A health halo refers to overestimating how healthy an item is based on a single feature, such as being low in calories, gluten -free or high in protein.
zh: health halo（健康光环）指的是仅凭某个单一特征——比如低卡路里、无麸质或高蛋白——就高估某食品的健康程度。

text: The idiom the tide is turning means that things are changing.
zh: 习语the tide is turning意思是事情正在发生变化。

text: The trend fibre -maxing means people intentionally consume lots of fibre by eating fibre -rich foods and supplements for their health benefits.
zh: “纤维最大化”这一潮流指的是人们有意通过食用富含纤维的食物和补充剂来大量摄入纤维，以获取健康益处。

text: And finally, a hack is a good solution or piece of advice.
zh: 最后，hack指的是一个好办法或好建议。

text: twice.
zh: 再说一遍。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up.
zh: 我们的六分钟又到时间了。

text: But here's another hack for you.
zh: 不过这里还有一个“hack”送给你。

text: Remember, you can find worksheets, quizzes and loads more resources to improve your English over on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 记住，你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到练习题、测验和更多提升英语的资源。

text: See you there soon, but for now, it's goodbye.
zh: 我们很快在那里见，不过现在，先说再见。

text: Goodbye.
zh: 再见。
